items.title: Good News in History, June 8
items.blurb: Also, 120 years ago today, President Teddy Roosevelt signed into law the Antiquities Act. The Act was intended to allow the President to set aside certain valuable public natural areas as park and conservation land. The 1906 act stated that it was intended for: “… the protection…

items.title: Endangered California Condor Flies into Oregon for the First Time in 122 Years
items.blurb: Last month, a California condor flew into Oregon before returning after several hundred miles to its home in Redwoods National Park, becoming the first condor recorded in the state since 1904. Taking a closer look, condor conservationists among northern California’s Yurok tribe…

items.title: Good News in History, June 7
items.blurb: 18 years ago today, Iceland enshrined the area surrounding Vatnajökull glacier as a national park, the second-largest in all of Europe behind Yugyd Va in Russia. The Vatnajökull glacier is the largest in Europe outside the Arctic, but the national park includes two areas that we…

items.title: Cancer Vaccine Produces 49% Melanoma Reduction in Patients Five Years Later
items.blurb: The combination of a vaccine and a drug, which both harness the immune system to attack cancer cells, has proven successful in cutting the risk of skin cancer recurrence and death by 49 percent, a new study shows. This reduction was calculated five years after patients had their…

items.title: Win a Free Wedding at Historic Hudson Valley Estate For Couple With the Best Story
items.blurb: A historic Hudson Valley estate built in 1895 is reopening as a wedding venue and retreat—and the owners, a husband-and-wife team, are inaugurating the restored property by giving away a free wedding to the couple who tells the best story. Pine Brook Manor, about 90 minutes from…

items.title: Nature’s Beauty is Restored After Farmer Obliterated Shoreline of Important Salmon Run
items.blurb: Before-and-after photos from Herefordshire, England, show how nature’s beauty has returned to a riverside previously obliterated by a local farmer. The farmer used an 18-ton digger to dredge a section of the River Lugg near Leominster, and stripped every tree from a mile-long st…

items.title: ‘Relentless Outreach’: The State That Doesn’t Give Up on Mentally Ill Residents
items.blurb: One Thanksgiving, while most families gathered around dinner tables, Janina Estrada cruised the neighborhoods of Orange, California, searching for her 42-year-old son Jimmy Barela in the rain. For nearly two decades, he had cycled through schizophrenia, addiction, diabetes, home…
